records.ety: [AS. grōwan; akin to D. groeijen, Icel. grōa, Dan. groe, Sw. gro. Cf. Green, Grass.]
records.morph: [imp. Grew (gr); p. p. Grown (grōn); p. pr. & vb. n. Growing.]

defs: To increase in size by a natural and organic process; to increase in bulk by the gradual assimilation of new matter into the living organism; -- said of animals and vegetables and their organs.
To increase in any way; to become larger and stronger; to be augmented; to advance; to extend; to wax; to accrue.
quotes: Winter began to grow fast on.
Even just the sum that I do owe to you Is growing to me by Antipholus.

defs: To spring up and come to maturity in a natural way; to be produced by vegetation; to thrive; to flourish; as, rice grows in warm countries.
To pass from one state to another; to result as an effect from a cause; to become; as, to grow pale.
quotes: For his mind Had grown Suspicion's sanctuary.

defs: To become attached or fixed; to adhere.
quotes: Our knees shall kneel till to the ground they grow.
These wars have grown out of commercial considerations.

defs: To cause to grow; to cultivate; to produce; as, to grow a crop; to grow wheat, hops, or tobacco.
